steps.title: Welcome to Day 23 — AI for Financial Planning
steps.body: Money can be stressful — especially when you are not sure where it goes or whether you are making the right decisions. Today you will learn how AI helps you budget better, analyze spending, set savings goals, and make smarter financial choices.

steps.title: AI as your financial assistant
steps.body: AI cannot manage your bank account or pay your bills. But it can do three powerful things: (1) Analyze your spending patterns and suggest where to cut. (2) Help you build a realistic budget that fits your life. (3) Explain financial concepts in plain language so you make better decisions. Think of AI as a friendly financial coach who never judges.

steps.title: Create your budget with AI

fields.label: What is your monthly income after tax?
What are your fixed monthly costs (rent, bills, subscriptions)?
What do you typically spend on groceries, dining, transport, and fun?

steps.template: I earn {{income}} per month. My fixed costs: {{fixed_costs}}. My flexible spending: {{flexible_spending}}. Help me create a realistic monthly budget. Show me: (1) Where I could save. (2) The 50/30/20 breakdown. (3) How much I could save each month for my goals.

steps.title: Save your budget
steps.body: Paste the budget AI created for you. You can adjust it later as your situation changes.

steps.title: Spending analysis — where does the money go?
steps.body: Most people have no idea where their money actually goes. Take a week's worth of expenses — even rough numbers — and ask AI: 'Here is what I spent in the last 7 days: [list]. Categorize these, tell me what surprises you, and suggest one simple change I could make.' Often the answer is something small: one coffee a day adds up to $1,000+ a year.

questions.label: How do you feel about your finances right now?
options: I prefer not to think about money

questions.label: What is the biggest financial question you would like AI to help you answer?

steps.title: Setting savings goals that stick
steps.body: A goal without a plan is just a wish. AI can turn 'I want to save more' into a concrete plan. Prompt: 'I want to save $[amount] by [date]. My monthly income is [X] and expenses are [Y]. Create a savings plan — tell me exactly how much to save each week and suggest specific spending cuts to make it possible.'

steps.title: My financial planning toolkit
items: I can create a monthly budget by entering my income and expenses into AI
I can analyze my spending patterns and spot easy savings
I can set a savings goal with a realistic timeline
I understand the 50/30/20 rule for budgeting
I can use AI to compare financial options like insurance or loans

steps.title: Financial planning check
steps.question: What is the 50/30/20 budgeting rule?
feedback: Correct. Needs get half, wants get 30%, and 20% goes to savings.
Close, but needs should come before wants in your budget.
Not quite — half your income should cover essential needs.

steps.title: Investment basics — no jargon
steps.body: AI is great at explaining investing without confusing jargon. Ask: 'Explain investing like I am 15 years old. What is a stock? What is compounding? How much would I need to start? And what is the safest way to begin?' The key insight: you do not need to be an expert to start. You just need to start.

steps.title: Build your financial plan

fields.label: What is a short-term financial goal (3-6 months)?
What is a long-term financial goal (1-5 years)?
fields.placeholder: save for a down payment on a house

fields.label: What is your biggest financial challenge right now?
fields.placeholder: impulse spending on takeout and shopping

steps.template: Help me create a financial plan. Short-term goal: {{short_term}}. Long-term goal: {{long_term}}. Biggest challenge: {{biggest_challenge}}. My monthly income and expenses from my budget. Create a step-by-step plan: (1) What to do this month. (2) What to do this quarter. (3) What habits to build. Keep it simple and realistic.

steps.title: Save your financial plan
steps.body: Paste the financial plan AI created. Review it monthly.

steps.title: Bill negotiation — yes, AI can help
steps.body: Did you know most bills are negotiable? Internet, phone, insurance, even rent. But most people avoid negotiating because it is awkward. AI can script the conversation for you. Prompt: 'I pay $[amount] for [service]. I have been a customer for [years]. Competitors offer [X]. Write a polite script I can use to ask for a lower rate.' You would be surprised how often it works.

questions.question: What is the 50/30/20 rule used for?
options: Budgeting your income across needs, wants, and savings

questions.question: How can AI help with bill negotiation?
options: AI calls the company for you
AI writes a polite script you can use to ask for a better rate

questions.question: You need a large amount of money and expertise to start investing.

steps.title: Day 23 complete — Financial Planner
steps.body: You can now use AI to budget, analyze spending, set savings goals, understand investing, and even negotiate bills. Tomorrow: AI for learning and skill development — accelerating how you learn anything.
